files.purpose: Entry point, CDN Phaser load, script load order
Game constants: dimensions, palette, scoring, enemy names/variants, localStorage keys
Procedural stage generation: attack windows, sequence length, HP, fakeouts, boss/rest stages
Web Audio API synth (kick/hihat/bass music, SFX for blocks/miss/death/combos) + ad stubs
GameEffects mixin: block juice, particles, arrows, urgency, pause overlay, stage clear celebration
Core GameScene: swipe input, block resolution, damage, death, stage progression, enemy HP
BootScene (SVG texture loading), Phaser config, visibility handler, touch prevention
Mobile-first responsive layout, dark background, landscape constraint

core_mechanic.core_loop: Arrow appears showing direction -> player swipes matching direction -> timing determines quality (perfect/good/late) -> enemy HP decreases -> repeat until enemy defeated -> next stage with harder enemy
core_mechanic.mastery_curve: Beginners learn 4-direction swiping and survive with late blocks. Experts chase perfect timing for combo multipliers (up to 4x), memorize fakeout patterns at stage 10+, and maintain combos through boss fights.
core_mechanic.satisfaction_source: Combo system with escalating pitch, growing combo text, camera zoom at 10+/20+ combos, and hitstop on perfect blocks create rhythmic flow state. Defeating enemies with HP bars provides closure.

mechanic_depth.reasoning: Four-direction swiping with timing windows. Perfect/good/late tiers and combo multiplier. Fakeouts after stage 10. Simple core verb with limited skill ceiling.

juice_quality.reasoning: Hitstop, camera shake, flash, colored particles, floating scores, combo milestone bursts, stage clear celebration, arrow animations, HP color shift, score bounce, enemy bounce. Procedural audio with pitch-scaled combo SFX.

content_variety.reasoning: 5 enemy variants with distinct colors but identical behavior. 30 enemy names. Boss/rest stages provide rhythm. No environmental changes, no special attacks, no power-ups.

difficulty_curve.reasoning: Attack window shrinks 800ms to 220ms. Sequence length 1 to 6. Fakeouts from stage 11. Boss stages tighter. Rest stages at every 10. Linear but well-engineered.

loop_tightness.reasoning: Death to retry in ~2-3 seconds. Stage transitions 800ms. Arrow-to-arrow 300ms. Punchy rhythm.

visual_appeal.reasoning: Dark theme with neon arrows readable and functional. SVG characters simplistic. No background art. Color-coded arrows are the visual highlight.

code_quality.reasoning: Clean 8-file architecture. Effects as mixin. Audio encapsulated. Stage generation pure functions. Proper BootScene texture loading. setTimeout for hitstop.

weaknesses.description: All enemy variants behave identically - only cosmetic color and HP/window differ
No spatial gameplay - player and enemy static images, no movement or dodging
No progression or meta-game beyond high score
Fakeouts start too late (stage 11), first 10 stages pure reaction
Combo multiplier caps at 4x with no further reward
Vibration toggle exists but never wired to navigator.vibrate()

mechanics.idea: Distinct enemy behaviors: Fast=shorter windows telegraph earlier, Tank=2 swipes per arrow, Tricky=direction change mid-attack, Boss=multi-directional combos
Counter-attack system: after perfect block, brief window to swipe for bonus damage
Special moves at combo thresholds: 10=area damage, 20=time slow, 30=instant kill
Simultaneous multi-directional attacks requiring rapid sequential swipes

feel.idea: Wire vibration: 15ms on perfect, 50ms on damage, 200ms on death
Swipe trail visualization with colored line and particle trail
Enemy reaction animations: flinch, stumble, taunt
Player stance animations: idle sway, block pose, damage stagger

content.idea: Belt/rank progression: White->Yellow->Green->Blue->Brown->Black belt with visual changes
Mini-boss special attacks with swipe sequences and projectile dodging
Power-up drops: shield, slow-time, double-points, life restore

meta.idea: Achievement system with persistent unlocks displayed on menu
Skill tree/permanent upgrades with dojo points

fun.idea: Rhythm mode: arrows sync to procedural music beat
Dramatic finishing blow: slow-motion zoom on last HP hit
Rage mode: brief invincibility after damage where all blocks are perfect

design.idea: Tutorial overlay for first play with animated hand gestures
Score breakdown on game over: accuracy, perfect/good/late/miss counts
Stage preview banner with enemy intro card

game_flow.0_5s: Dark screen with SWIPE DOJO title, floating particles, player preview, pulsing TAP TO PLAY button.
game_flow.5_15s: First enemy appears with name and HP bar. First arrow spawns. 800ms window is generous. Immediate audio+visual feedback on block.
game_flow.15_30s: First enemy defeated (3 HP). Death burst + celebration. Stage 2 begins. Combo counter builds.
game_flow.30_60s: Stages 3-5. Windows tighten. Sequences grow to 2 arrows. Boss at stage 5. Lives become relevant.
game_flow.60s_plus: Stages 6+: longer sequences. Stage 11+: fakeouts. Pure reaction test. Most die stages 8-15. Same visuals throughout.

evolution_potential.recommended_focus: Strong juice and tight core loop but needs mechanical variety. Priority: 1) distinct enemy behaviors, 2) belt/rank progression, 3) counter-attack or special moves for combo payoff.
